Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Criminal Code and the Corrections and Conditional Release Act (conditions of release and conditional sentences)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Criminal Code and the Corrections and Conditional Release Act (conditions of release and conditional sentences)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Criminal Code and the Corrections and Conditional Release Act to create a new offence for the breach of conditions of conditional release imposed in relation to certain serious offences and to require the reporting of those breaches to the appropriate authorities.
It also amends the Criminal Code to preclude persons convicted of certain offences from serving their sentence in the community.
His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: Section 145 of the Criminal Code is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Failure to comply with a condition
Subsection.Text: Every person who is released on conditional release in relation to a conviction for an offence set out in Schedule I or II of the Corrections and Conditional Release Act and who fails or refuses to comply, without reasonable excuse, with a condition of parole or statutory release or a condition of a temporary absence is guilty of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an indictable offence and is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding two years; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offence punishable on summary conviction.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 742.1(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the offence is not an offence, prosecuted by way of indictment, for which the maximum term of imprisonment is 14 years or life;

Subsection.Text: Section 742.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the offence is not an offence, prosecuted by way of indictment, for which the maximum term of imprisonment is 10 years, that

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: involved the import, export, trafficking or production of drugs, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: involved the use of a weapon; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the offence is not an offence, prosecuted by way of indictment, under any of the following provisions:

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: section 281 (abduction of person under age of 14),

Subparagraph.Label: (ix)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 348(1)(e) (breaking and entering a place other than a dwelling-house),

Subparagraph.Label: (x)
Subparagraph.Text: section 349 (being unlawfully in a dwelling-house), and

Section.Text: The Corrections and Conditional Release Act is amended by adding the following after the heading âSuspension, Termination, Revocation and Inoperativeness of Parole, Statutory Release or Long-Term Supervisionâ before section 135:

If it comes to the knowledge of a parole supervisor that an offender who is released on conditional release or under a long-term supervision order in relation to a conviction for an offence set out in Schedule I or II has committed a breach of a condition of parole or statutory release, a condition of a long-term supervision order issued under the Criminal Code, a condition referred to in section 134.1 or a condition of a temporary absence, the parole supervisor shall inform the Parole Board of Canada and the Service, and the Attorney General and police force that have jurisdiction in the place in which the breach of the condition occurred, of the breach and the circumstances surrounding the breach.
